action_items.task: Keep track of extra income from day labor for reporting purposes
action_items.context: Client is working under-the-table day labor shifts to pay an electric bill.
Required to obtain a DOT medical card for trucking academy.
Required to maintain healthy blood pressure levels to pass the DOT physical.
Routine follow-up meeting; client already requested the morning off from the Co-op.

critical_updates.details: Client continues to work 35 hours a week at the Co-op but has started picking up under-the-table day labor shifts on construction sites to earn quick cash for a utility bill.

Client received a shut-off notice for a $280 electric bill. He applied for LIHEAP online on Tuesday but is working extra day labor shifts to cover the bill while waiting for the application to process.
WIOA fully funded the client's tuition for Tri-State Trucking Academy. He will begin evening classes on the 28th to pursue his CDL.
Client reports taking his Lisinopril every morning without missing a day to manage his blood pressure, which is necessary to pass his upcoming DOT physical.
Client reports no police contact or legal issues since the last visit.
Client reports no alcohol or drug use, stating he is remaining focused on getting his CDL.

next_episode_context: The client is maintaining 35 hours a week at the Co-op and is currently working day labor to cover a $280 electric shut-off notice, for which he has also applied for LIHEAP. WIOA fully funded his tuition for Tri-State Trucking Academy, and he begins evening classes on the 28th. He is scheduled for a DOT physical next Thursday and is compliant with his Lisinopril medication. No legal or substance issues reported. Next office visit is scheduled for the 12th at 9:00 AM.
